content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

December 23, 1976

Frederick J. Whisman
Executive Officer
San Francisco County Superior
Court
City Hall 480
San Francisco, CA  94102

Dear Mr. Whisman:

This letter is in response to your letter of December 16, 1976, regarding the reporting requirements of the Political Reform Act.

Please be advised that the Commission's staff's interim advice is that benefits received from state or local government agencies, such as retirement and pension funds, are not reportable as income.

Since the reporting requirements, of income from retirement and pension funds, are not clearly defined, the Commission will consider the subject next year. However, until otherwise advised, you may rely on the interim advice.

If you have any further questions, please contact this office.

Sincerely,


Dawn Wiser
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

December 29, 1976


Mr. Frederick J. Whisman
Executive Officer
Superior Court of California
City Hall 430
San Francisco, California 94104

Dear Mr. Whisman:

Thank you for your letter of December 16 concerning whether judges
are required to report benefits received from either a California
state or county retirement fund as income on their Statement of
Economic Interests.

The Commission is currently reviewing the question of whether or
not payments or benefits received from pension and retirement funds
are reportable as income on the financial disclosure statement.

In lieu of an official opinion rendered by the Commission, the
staff advise to filers of Form 721 is that pension or retirement
benefit payments from public (i.e. federal, state or local government) sources need not be disclosed but payments of a similar type
from private sector benefit plans should be reported.

If the Commission's decision on the question is contrary to the
interim advice, you will be notified. However, anyone who files a
statement conforming with the interim advice will be deemed to be
in compliance with the law.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

Superior Court of California

San Francisco

December 16, 1976

FREDERICK J WHISMAN
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
CITY HALL 480
(415) 558-3169

Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

For the information and assistance of some
members of this Court, will you please provide us
with an answer to the following question:

Is it required that a judge include in his
Statement of Economic Interests (Form 720) any bene-
fits received from either a California state or
county retirement fund?

This office has been informed that such
benefits are considered earned annuity rather than
income and therefore not reportable.

We would appreciate verification of that
information.

Very truly yours,

FREDERICK J. WHISMAN
Executive Officer

FJ:mm
